rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees By Petitioners And Judgement Creditors
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, BABAC DOANE Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees By Petitioners And Judgement Creditors is GRANTED in the amount of $200,000 per stipulation of the parties. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, BABAC DOANE Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment is GRANTED. Petitioners met their burden of establishing that the Proposed Judgment Debtors had control of the underlying litigation and were virtually represented in that proceeding, that there is a unity of interest between the Original and Proposed Judgment Debtors, and an inequitable result would follow if the entities are treated as distinct. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: BRETT MILLIER, AS SUCCESSOR CO-TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL VS. MARC DANA MERKER, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Referees Motion For Final Distribution Of Proceeds Of Sale, For Authority To Make Distribution, For Referee Fees And Costs, Request To Maintain A Closing Reserve, And Request To Be Discharged As Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2024 line 2. OTHER AMY HARRINGTON (PARTITION REFEREE) Notice Of Motion And Referees Motion For Final Distribution Of Proceeds Of Sale, For Authority To Make Distribution, For Referee Fees And Costs, Request To Maintain A Closing Reserve, And Request To Be Discharged As Referee is OFF CALENDAR, premature. Referee seeks approval on the final distribution of proceeds including Plaintiff's attorney's fees. On August 25, 2024 Plaintiff was authorized to make a motion for determination and apportionment of the fees per paragraph G of February 20, 2024 order. Court's record does not reflect any such motion or any ruling on such motion. February 20, 2024 Order did not authorize the Referee to make such motion or to include this request for distribution in referee's other motions. On October 17, 2024 this Court gave Plaintiff an opportunity to properly file a Motion for Attorney's fees to be heard in conjunction with this motion. Plaintiff chose to file a motion that does not conform to the California Rules of Court. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRETT MILLIER, AS SUCCESSOR CO-TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL VS. MARC DANA MERKER, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: / Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Award Of $91,313.16 In Attorney Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Robert Peter Weiss
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF BRETT MILLIER, AS SUCCESSOR CO-TRUSTEE OF THE PHILLIP A. INGALLS AND ELIZABETH CANDLISH FAMILY TRUST 1999, DATED APRIL 13, 1999, HEATHER STRAUSS, AS SUCCESSOR CO-TRUSTEE OF THE / Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Award Of $91,313.16 In Attorney Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Robert Peter Weiss is OFF CALENDAR. The Notice of Motion fails to comply with CRC 3.1110 as no grounds for the issuance of the order are listed in the Notice of Motion. The Court also notes that the Points and Authorities appear to rely, inter alia, on CC 1717 for the award of attorney's fees. There is no indication anywhere in the brief how this award is tethered to any causes of action in the complaint or the prayer therein. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALISON THOMAS VS. ERNEST ANTONIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT ERNEST ANTONIS, MAKRAS REAL ESTATE, VICTOR MAKRAS DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1010.6(a)(3)(B). Additionally, the demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is not accompanied by a declaration re: meet and confer efforts as to the First Amended Complaint. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in-person or over the phone at a mutually agreeable time no later than December 13, 2024. If parties can not agree on a date a time, parties shall meet and confer on December 5, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. No later than December 16, 2024 parties to file a declaration attesting to their good faith participation in meet and confer efforts. Defendant may file a demurrer if necessary after the meet and confer no later than December 17, 2024. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ADAM FAGEL ET AL VS. OYVIND SEAN KINSEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment:And Quash Service Of Summons (Ccp Section 418.10); Memo Of Points And Authorities;
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT ONERENT INC. DBA POPLAR HOMES Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment:And Quash Service Of Summons (Ccp Section 418.10); Memo Of Points And Authorities; is GRANTED in part. Hearing Required in part. Motion to Vacate Default is granted based on CCP 473(b). Hearing Required as to the Motion to Quash. No later than November 27, 2024 by 9:00 a.m. Nicole Cheatham (purported agent for Service of Process) shall file a declaration attesting that she was available for service at 10080 N Wolfe Rd SW3-200 Cupertino, CA 95014 between June 7, 2024 and September 4, 2024. Ms. Cheatham to state in the declaration whether she was present in-person at the premises during business hours every business day during this period and available for personal service; or for the days that she was off-site, who in the office was authorized to be sub-served on behalf of the corporation. Declarant to describe the set-up of the office: is there a receptionist; is business open to public to walk-in, etc. Ms. Cheatham to be present at the hearing. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. AS TRUSTEE FOR HARBORVIEW VS. ANGIE ROYCE CHEATHAM-SANDERS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT ETHAN HERZ DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is continued to December 6, 2024 for Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief and declaration addressing why Plaintiff's late-filed opposition served by mail should be considered. The brief shall not exceed 2-pages. Brief and declaration to be filed and served no later than December 2, 2024. The Court notes that this is not the first time Plaintiff's opposition is filed late. rulings.Case Title: ADJE C. LASSEY VS. DARRYL PERKINS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 27, 2024 line 9. DEFENDANT DARRYL PERKINS, KAREN PERKINS, DERRICK PERKINS, SELASCIE SMITH DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective. It fails to provide a warning to the tenant regarding the requirement to submit a s statement with supporting evidence to the landlord as to the claims under SFRO 37.9(j). The Court does not consider a trial court decision in case CUD-24-675798, as Defendant provided no authority for such consideration. The Court does not consider exhibits attached directly to the opposition brief, as opposing party provided no authority for such consideration. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
